notableFindings: V2 learned hit/stay plus threat targeting was strong, but uploaded EV hit/stay was a small improvement when fused with our threat targeting.
Hidden-state target rollout oracle showed large upside, but deployable belief rollout did not beat hand threat targeting.
Fresh win/loss-only league CEM learned nontrivial play but did not beat engineered production policy in multiplayer pools.
Column/triplet/end-risk V2 improved substantially over threshold bots.
The uploaded strategy policy was stronger than V2 in multiplayer and was temporarily promoted.
Solo board-efficiency and reveal-geometry training transferred extremely well to multiplayer and produced the final hard bot.
A polishing multi-player run from the solo-inspired policy improved it further.
